metadata.title: Receipt Extraction Pipeline: Schema-First vs. No-Schema

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: The side-by-side sold me: the no-schema pass raised zero exception flags while the schema-driven pass surfaced a foreign currency, a handwritten total, and an ambiguous line item, and the over-500-dollar rule routed high-value receipts to a human. I want that on my own receipts. Take a batch I'll paste and (1) extract with a strict schema, setting missing or ambiguous fields to null and listing them in exception flags, (2) add an exception rule that flags any total over a threshold I set for human review, and (3) show which receipts the pipeline stops on instead of silently deciding.

props.command: The side-by-side sold me: the no-schema pass raised zero exception flags while the schema-driven pass surfaced a foreign currency, a handwritten total, and an ambiguous line item, and the over-500-dollar rule routed high-value receipts to a human. I want that on my own receipts. Take a batch I'll paste and (1) extract with a strict schema, setting missing or ambiguous fields to null and listing them in exception flags, (2) add an exception rule that flags any total over a threshold I set for human review, and (3) show which receipts the pipeline stops on instead of silently deciding.
props.segment: Receipt Extraction Pipeline: Schema-First vs. No-Schema

beats.narration_text: Without a schema, you're not extracting data — you're hoping for consistency.

Receipt extraction without a schema produces output — but field names vary, dates come in mixed formats, totals and subtotals get confused, and ambiguous cases get resolved silently with no indication a decision was made. The schema is not a constraint; it's a contract that makes the output auditable.

Extract from this receipt: vendor as string, date in ISO 8601, total amount as float, line items as a list, currency as 3-letter code. If any field is ambiguous or missing, set it to null and add to exception flags.

props.command: claude "Extract from this receipt: vendor (string), date (ISO 8601), total_amount (float), line_items (list of {description, quantity, unit_price}), currency (3-letter code). If any field is ambiguous or missing, set it to null and add to exception_flags list." < receipt.txt

beats.narration_text: The extraction script runs two passes: no-schema then schema-driven, and produces a side-by-side comparison highlighting divergent cells.

5 receipts processed. No-schema: 3 date format inconsistencies, 2 total/subtotal confusions, 0 exception flags raised. Schema-driven: dates all ISO 8601, totals correctly separated, 3 exception flags raised — one foreign currency, one handwritten total, one ambiguous line item. The schema did not just improve the output — it made the uncertainty visible.
Add exception rule: if total is over 500 dollars, flag for human review.

props.command: claude "Re-extract with this additional rule: if total_amount > 500, add high_value_flag: true to the output." < receipts_batch.txt

beats.narration_text: Two receipts above the 500-dollar threshold: both get high_value_flag true in structured output. Downstream automation routes them to human approval queue. The AI does not approve high-value receipts — it flags them and stops. The exception rule moved the accountability where it belongs.

The schema is an interface contract for extraction. Without it, ambiguities are silently resolved by the model. With it, ambiguities surface as exception flags — which is the only outcome that preserves human accountability for the decisions.
Next: audit your workspace permissions — which access does each task actually need?
Nik Bear Brown. Brutalist and Educational AI. nikbearbrown.com.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Receipt Extraction Pipeline: Schema-First vs. No-Schema] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
